mission.title: Install and Configure vLLM
mission.description: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for large language models (LLMs). It is ideal for scenarios requiring fast and scalable LLM serving, particularly in production environments. Key trade-offs include the need for GPU resources and potential complexity in configuration.

steps.title: Add Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the vLLM Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install vLLM easily.

```bash

helm repo add vllm https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/releases/download/v0.18.0

```

> This adds the specific version of the vLLM chart.

steps.title: Install vLLM
steps.description:
This command installs vLLM in the specified namespace, setting up the necessary resources for serving LLMs.

```bash

helm install vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --create-namespace --version 0.18.0

```

> Ensure the namespace is created for isolation.

steps.title: Create a Custom Resource for vLLM
steps.description:
This step creates a custom resource (CR) that defines the model and configuration for vLLM serving.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1alpha1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: my-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  replicas: 2
EOF

```

> Replace 'gpt-3' with your desired model.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1alpha1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: my-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  replicas: 2
EOF

steps.title: Verify vLLM Deployment
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the vLLM deployment to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n vllm

```

> Look for the status 'Running' for all pods.

uninstall.title: Uninstall vLLM
uninstall.description:
This step removes the vLLM deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all data associated with the deployment.

```bash

helm uninstall vllm --namespace vllm

kubectl delete namespace vllm

```

> This will remove the release and all associated resources.
> This will delete the namespace and any remaining resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade vLLM
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your configurations and data. This process includes draining the pods, upgrading, and verifying the installation.

```bash

kubectl drain <pod-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --version 0.18.0

kubectl get pods -n vllm

```

> Replace <pod-name> with the name of your vLLM pod.
> This command upgrades vLLM to the specified version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image may not exist or is inaccessible.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure it is available in the specified registry.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM pod is crashing due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Review the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n vllm' and adjust the configuration accordingly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough CPU resources available for the vLLM pods.

**Fix:**
Scale up your cluster or adjust the resource requests in the vLLM configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Service Unavailable'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM service is not reachable, possibly due to misconfigured service or network policies.

**Fix:**
Check the service configuration and ensure the correct ports are exposed.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new model types and improved memory management features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the vLLM pods running with the output: 'Running' status when executing 'kubectl get pods -n vllm'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure your Kubernetes cluster meets the resource requirements and you have the necessary tools installed.
